commits.author: chr1syy

commits.message:
Fix shield rep test: pass absolute path as File name for jsdom compat

jsdom's File/Blob implementation (v23) does not expose text(),
arrayBuffer(), or stream() methods. readLogText() therefore falls
through to its Node.js filesystem fallback, which resolves the file
by file.name. The old helper passed only the basename
("test_shield_reps.txt"), causing a silent ENOENT and an empty parse
result (0 rep-received entries).

Fix: pass the full absolute FIXTURE_PATH as the File name so the
Node.js fallback resolves correctly via isAbsolute() -> direct read.
All 183 tests pass (11 test files).

commits.author: chr1syy

commits.message:
MAESTRO: Refactor version.ts caching, harden changelog limit param, parameterize changelog script

- version.ts: compute git commit/tag/build-time once at module load; prefer
  GIT_SHA / GIT_TAG / BUILD_TIME env vars (injected at Docker build time) before
  falling back to execSync / new Date()
- Dockerfile: expose GIT_SHA, GIT_TAG, BUILD_TIME as ARG + ENV in the runtime
  stage so the baked values are available at container startup
- release.yml: add "Set build metadata" step to capture BUILD_TIME; pass
  GIT_SHA, GIT_TAG, BUILD_TIME as build-args to docker/build-push-action;
  add "Generate changelog" step with GITHUB_TOKEN env var for authenticated
  GitHub API calls in CI
- changelog/route.ts: replace bare parseInt with NaN-safe parsing that falls
  back to the default limit of 50 when the query param is invalid
- generate-changelog.js: resolve repo from CHANGELOG_REPO / GITHUB_REPOSITORY
  env vars (falling back to chr1syy/eve-log-parser); add Authorization header
  when GITHUB_TOKEN is present; build COMMITS_URL from resolved REPO constant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: chr1syy

- Dockerfile: add `RUN apk add --no-cache curl` in runtime stage so HEALTHCHECK curl command is available
- src/app/api/changelog/route.ts: remove duplicate CommitEntry/ChangelogResponse interfaces; import from lib/types instead
- src/__tests__/integration/changelog.test.tsx: add `import type { ReactNode } from "react"` and replace React.ReactNode with ReactNode in mock provider
- src/app/damage-dealt/page.tsx: wrap handleTargetClick with useCallback([]) and add it to engagementColumns useMemo dep array to prevent stale closures

fix: address Copilot PR review comments

  avoid sort-order dependency (results are sorted by totalDamage desc)
- damageTaken.ts: derive shipType from misses fallback
  (damage[0]?.shipType ?? misses[0]?.shipType) so all-miss groups retain
  attacker ship type

  to engagementColumns useMemo dep array to satisfy exhaustive-deps

  bare T assertion to reflect that .find() may return undefined

commits.message:
docs: add comprehensive README, LICENSE, and CONTRIBUTING guides

Add professional-grade documentation to the EVE Log Parser project:

- README.md: Complete project overview with features, quick start, project structure,
  development guide, testing info, troubleshooting, roadmap, and community links.
  Replaces the generic Next.js bootstrap template with project-specific content.

- LICENSE: MIT license with third-party acknowledgments, EVE Online trademark notice,
  and warranty disclaimer to clarify fan-work status.

- CONTRIBUTING.md: Comprehensive contributor guide including:
  * Setup instructions and development workflow
  * Coding standards (TypeScript, React, CSS, naming conventions)
  * Testing guidelines with examples
  * Conventional commit message format
  * Full PR process walkthrough
  * Issue reporting guidelines
  * Architecture patterns for extending parser/analysis modules
  * Performance considerations and resources

These updates establish clear expectations for contributors, improve onboarding for
new developers, and present the project professionally for open-source distribution.
